steps.title: Find the shared cassava claim
steps.content: Text 1 says Portuguese traders brought cassava from Brazil to the West African coast in the 1500s, but cassava remained relatively unknown to most of the region until the 1800s. Text 2 also describes cassava's adoption into West African diets as slow. The texts give different explanations, but both agree that cassava became important long after it was introduced.

steps.title: Eliminate unsupported claims
steps.content:
• Choice B — Several of the most commonly grown crops in West Africa are originally from Brazil.: Only cassava is discussed, so neither text supports a claim about several crops.
  • Choice C — The climate of the West African coast in the 1500s prevented cassava's spread in the region.: Neither text mentions climate. Text 1 cites limited contact between coastal and inland communities, and Text 2 cites the need for preparation knowledge.
  • Choice D — The most commonly used methods to cook cassava today date to the 1500s.: Text 2 mentions preparation knowledge, but neither text describes current cooking methods or dates them to the 1500s.
Choice A matches the shared point that cassava did not become significant in West Africa until long after its introduction. The answer is A.
